articles.title: GEMINI PREPS MARKUP EDITING FOR NANO BANANA IMAGES
articles.summary: Google is testing a built-in markup tool in Gemini that lets users highlight parts of AI-generated images and request targeted edits directly inside the chat, removing the need for external editors. The feature would enable faster, more precise region-based edits and support Google's goal of making Gemini a more seamless multimodal assistant.

articles.title: MACBOOK NEO IS NOW OFFICIAL: A18 PRO CHIP, $599, FOUR COLORS, MORE
articles.summary: Apple introduced the MacBook Neo, a new budget laptop starting at $599 powered by the iPhone-derived A18 Pro chip with a 13-inch display and up to 16 hours of battery life. It comes in four colors and offers 8GB RAM, 256GB–512GB storage, two USB-C ports, and optional Touch ID on the higher-end model. Educational pricing starts at $499.

articles.title: CANVAS IN AI MODE LAUNCHES FOR EVERYONE IN THE US
DESIGN SYSTEM CONTRIBUTIONS WORK BETTER WHEN EVERYONE KNOWS YOUR NAME
articles.summary: Design system contributions work better in small organizations where team members know each other personally, whereas in larger companies, complex processes make contributions ineffective. Contribution models should be simple enough to describe in a few paragraphs, relying on established relationships and trust rather than elaborate procedures. In small teams, a basic two-step process works best: create a recipe for the shared library, then optionally review it with the design system team for potential inclusion.

articles.title: THE JOY OF BUILDING SLOW
articles.summary: Five years ago, the author left a cushy job to start Not Boring Software, aiming to replace boring daily software with apps that make users feel something and want to spend time using them. Rather than following the typical tech industry script of rapid growth and scaling, the company chose to build slowly and sustainably, creating weather apps, timers, calculators, and other tools. This approach trades an infinitesimal chance at massive success for a real opportunity at meaningful, lasting success.

articles.title: WHY THE CREATIVE INDUSTRY FEELS BROKEN RIGHT NOW (AND WHY IT ISN'T)
articles.summary: The creative design industry has shifted from a vibrant, creator-led field in the 1980s to a corporatized system where holding companies, weakened design education, austerity, and cost-cutting have devalued creativity and worsened working conditions. Despite this decline, growing dissatisfaction is fueling a new wave of independent, creator-led agencies and a potential renaissance in creative work.

articles.title: AI VIDEO GENERATOR AND EDITOR IN YOUR BROWSER
articles.summary: Generate and refine visuals directly in the timeline, then add voice, captions, and polish without switching tools.

articles.title: ACCELERATE YOUR 3D WORKFLOW
articles.summary: Secret Sauce 3D is an AI co-pilot built for 3D artists and 3D enthusiasts. Generate high-poly starting points, segment into parts, optimize geometry, create UVs, and build texture bases — all with editable outputs at every step.

articles.title: SVG STUDIO
articles.summary: Inspect, edit, and optimize SVGs in the browser.

articles.title: DESIGN SYSTEMS CAN'T AUTOMATE AWAY ALL OF YOUR ACCESSIBILITY CONSIDERATIONS
articles.summary: Accessible design systems dramatically reduce remediation work, but automated checkers alone can't catch every issue. Critical gaps include misused components, unmanaged keyboard focus, color-only status indicators, poor text labels, and illogical heading structures. Real accessibility requires human judgment, disabled user testing, and attention to the full end-user experience beyond component-level concerns.

articles.title: CONFIGURATION COLLAPSE
articles.summary: Figma components with excessive props create a scrollable "configuration collapse" where too many visibility and layout options make components fragile and hard to maintain. Native Figma slots will replace many props with a composable design, allowing designers to build components through creative composition rather than endless switches and variants. This shift requires dropping complex props in favor of leaner component APIs that retain only essential behavioral properties, such as state, appearance, and size.

articles.title: TRAINING DESIGN JUDGMENT, HOW TO READ PRODUCTS LIKE A SENIOR DESIGNER
articles.summary: As AI makes design execution fast and accessible to anyone, the real competitive advantage for designers is no longer producing UI, but exercising judgment—understanding context, trade-offs, and user behavior behind design decisions. Strong design judgment, built through deliberate practice and analyzing products at multiple layers (surface, structure, and intent), is what allows designers to use AI effectively and remain valuable.

articles.title: BOLD ABSTRACT GEOMETRIC VECTOR SHAPES FOR ADOBE ILLUSTRATOR
articles.summary: Bold abstract geometric vector shapes by designer Oleg R. feature vibrant neon colors against black backgrounds, creating optical illusions of movement through strategic gradient placement.

articles.title: IF WE WANT A FAIRER CREATIVE INDUSTRY, WE NEED TO REDESIGN THE DOORWAY
articles.summary: The gap between academic and professional creative work creates a valuable learning opportunity, as students must shift from designing for grades to creating work that survives real-world commercial pressures.

articles.title: HOW OSCAR-NOMINATED BUTTERFLY USED AN UNUSUAL TECHNIQUE TO ACHIEVE ITS BEAUTIFUL ART STYLE
articles.summary: Florence Miailhe's Oscar-nominated animated short, Papillon (Butterfly), tells the life story of Holocaust survivor and Olympic swimmer Alfred Nakache through a painterly animation style that uses water and color to convey memory, trauma, and resilience.
